query:
You are working on the wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 23.01 (commit: 361bc30a).

## Issue to Resolve

CompletionHandlerException: Exception in resume onCancellation handler for CancellableContinuation(DispatchedContinuation[Dis...
Sentry Issue: [JETPACK-ANDROID-92S](https://a8c.sentry.io/issues/4295575735/?referrer=github_integration)

```
IllegalStateException: This mutex is not locked
    at kotlinx.coroutines.sync.MutexImpl.unlock(Mutex.kt:207)
    at kotlinx.coroutines.sync.MutexImpl$CancellableContinuationWithOwner$tryResume$token$1.invoke(Mutex.kt:255)
    at kotlinx.coroutines.sync.MutexImpl$CancellableContinuationWithOwner$tryResume$token$1.invoke(Mutex.kt:252)
    at kotlinx.coroutines.CancellableContinuationImpl.callOnCancellation(CancellableContinuationImpl.kt:259)
    at kotlinx.coroutines.CompletedContinuation.invokeHandlers(CancellableContinuationImpl.kt:659)
...
(8 additional frame(s) were not displayed)

CompletionHandlerException: Exception in resume onCancellation handler for CancellableContinuation(DispatchedContinuation[Dispatchers.IO, Continuation at org.wordpress.android.ui.uploads.UploadStarter$upload$2.invokeSuspend(UploadStarter.kt:211)@df58903]){Completed}@c9c7080
    at kotlinx.coroutines.CancellableContinuationImpl.callOnCancellation(CancellableContinuationImpl.kt:264)
    at kotlinx.coroutines.CompletedContinuation.invokeHandlers(CancellableContinuationImpl.kt:659)
    at kotlinx.coroutines.CancellableContinuationImpl.cancelCompletedResult$kotlinx_coroutines_core(CancellableContinuationImpl.kt:180)
    at kotlinx.coroutines.DispatchedTask.run(DispatchedTask.kt:100)
    at kotlinx.coroutines.internal.LimitedDispatcher$Worker.run(LimitedDispatcher.kt:115)
...
(5 additional frame(s) were not displayed)
```

This is not a duplicate of #17463 since this is now happening on coroutines 1.7.1 which fixed the underlying root cause of that other ticket. This has a similar stacktrace, but it looks like we might be misusing the Mutex in our `UploadStarter` implementation.

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

**TLDR:**¬†I think our code is silently making the crash look like itвАЩs an internal Mutex problem but in reality, we might be calling¬†`unlock`¬†unnecessarily when the Mutex internals are not expecting it. We should remove¬†[this line](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/blob/1b5e1f3fc97e13d0068ea803d5df7777f421180b/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/uploads/UploadStarter.kt#L186)¬†and I¬†think¬†it might solve our issues on coroutines 1.7.1.

Based on the¬†[breadcrumbs](https://a8c.sentry.io/issues/4295575735/?project=5731682&referrer=issue-stream&sort=freq&statsPeriod=14d&stream_index=1#breadcrumbs) I believe the actual issue is that we tried to¬†[start the post upload service](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/blob/1b5e1f3fc97e13d0068ea803d5df7777f421180b/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/uploads/UploadStarter.kt#L175-L179)¬†while the app was in the background and the system didnвАЩt let us, which caused all¬†`upload`¬†jobs (created¬†[here](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/blob/1b5e1f3fc97e13d0068ea803d5df7777f421180b/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/uploads/UploadStarter.kt#L138-L142)) to be canceled since the¬†[Parent Job](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/blob/1b5e1f3fc97e13d0068ea803d5df7777f421180b/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/uploads/UploadStarter.kt#L68) is not a¬†`SupervisorJob`.

This made all jobs (except the first one which failed because of an¬†`IllegalStateException`)¬†[fall here](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/blob/1b5e1f3fc97e13d0068ea803d5df7777f421180b/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/uploads/UploadStarter.kt#L183-L187)¬†(as can be seen in the breadcrumb logs).

**What I first thought could be happening is:**

We use the¬†`bgDispatcher`¬†([Dispatchers.Default](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/blob/e03d208247c7487a7497f873d91f6b0e52a3622e/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/modules/ThreadModule.kt#L40-L42)) which has several threads in the pool and we use the same¬†`mutex`¬†for all upload jobs. Because of that, multiple coroutines (running in different threads) are suspended awaiting the to¬†[acquire](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Semaphore.kt#L196-L201C6)¬†the semaphore (which¬†[creates a queue of segments](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Semaphore.kt#L285-L315)).

Since multiple jobs are being canceled at once, I¬†think¬†that the cancellation was not able to be properly propagated and the¬†`segment`¬†state for one of them was not marked as¬†`CANCELLED`¬†making the¬†`Semaphore#tryResumeNextFromQueue`¬†([which is¬†called on](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/1.7.1/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Semaphore.kt#L265)¬†`Semaphore#release`¬†[via the](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Mutex.kt#L216)¬†`Mutex#unlock`)¬†[call the](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Semaphore.kt#L329-L341)¬†`tryResumeAcquire`. Which in turn¬†[calls](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Semaphore.kt#L347C23-L347C23)¬†`Mutex#tryResume`¬†but since that is canceled it runs the¬†`onCancellation`¬†[callback](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Mutex.kt#L253-L255)¬†when trying to resume the underlying continuation, which is¬†[where the crash is happening](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Mutex.kt#L250-L262).

But I am not 100% sure about that, as I feel like this scenario would be pretty common for a synchronization mechanism using coroutines (like `Mutex` and `Semaphore`), which is why I believe that¬†`onCancellation`¬†callback also¬†unlocks¬†the `Mutex`, in case of that late propagation of the cancellation to the mutex¬†`Segment`.

**So, considering that the code makes sense and works (hopefully) what I¬†think¬†might be the issue is:**

We also have a¬†[call to](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/blob/1b5e1f3fc97e13d0068ea803d5df7777f421180b/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/uploads/UploadStarter.kt#L186)¬†`unlock`¬†on our side¬†and from the breadcrumbs we know that the code is reaching that line. So it might be that this¬†`unlock`¬†is actually unlocking the mutex¬†**at the same time as**¬†the whole cancellation flow I mentioned above is happening (_which would be possible because of the multiple threads in the Dispatcher pool_), so when the cancelation reaches the¬†`onCancellation`¬†[callback here](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Mutex.kt#L253-L255) our code already unlocked it, causing the crash to happen in the¬†`Mutex`¬†internals.

This is my best guess, but the Mutex/Semaphore code is a bit complex and I didnвАЩt fully understand it yet (and probably never will¬†рЯШЖ).

### Suggestion
Remove that `unlock`¬†[call ASAP](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/blob/1b5e1f3fc97e13d0068ea803d5df7777f421180b/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/uploads/UploadStarter.kt#L186)¬†as we really donвАЩt need it since we use¬†`withLock`¬†which is better in 2 ways:
1. It makes sure to¬†`unlock`¬†when exceptions happen ([try/finally](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Mutex.kt#L120-L131))
2. It only calls¬†`unlock`¬†if it was successfully locked before running the¬†`action`, so if my hypothesis above is correct, the internal¬†`lockSuspend`¬†code will cause a cancellation before reaching that¬†`try/finally`¬†block

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/uploads/UploadStarter.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

conversation_history.content:
1вЖТpackage org.wordpress.android.ui.uploads
  2вЖТ
  3вЖТimport android.content.Context
  4вЖТimport androidx.lifecycle.DefaultLifecycleObserver
  5вЖТimport androidx.lifecycle.LifecycleOwner
  6вЖТimport androidx.lifecycle.LiveData
  7вЖТimport androidx.lifecycle.Observer
  8вЖТimport androidx.lifecycle.ProcessLifecycleOwner
  9вЖТimport kotlinx.coroutines.CancellationException
 10вЖТimport kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineDispatcher
 11вЖТimport kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineScope
 12вЖТimport kotlinx.coroutines.Job
 13вЖТimport kotlinx.coroutines.async
 14вЖТimport kotlinx.coroutines.coroutineScope
 15вЖТimport kotlinx.coroutines.launch
 16вЖТimport kotlinx.coroutines.sync.Mutex
 17вЖТimport kotlinx.coroutines.sync.withLock
 18вЖТimport org.wordpress.android.analytics.AnalyticsTracker.Stat
 19вЖТimport org.wordpress.android.fluxc.Dispatcher
 20вЖТimport org.wordpress.android.fluxc.generated.UploadActionBuilder
 21вЖТimport org.wordpress.android.fluxc.model.SiteModel
 22вЖТimport org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.PageStore
 23вЖТimport org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.PostStore
 24вЖТimport org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.SiteStore
 25вЖТimport org.wordpress.android.modules.BG_THREAD
 26вЖТimport org.wordpress.android.modules.IO_THREAD
 27вЖТimport org.wordpress.android.testing.OpenForTesting
 28вЖТimport org.wordpress.android.ui.uploads.UploadActionUseCase.UploadAction
 29вЖТimport org.wordpress.android.ui.uploads.UploadActionUseCase.UploadAction.DO_NOTHING
 30вЖТimport org.wordpress.android.util.AppLog
 31вЖТimport org.wordpress.android.util.AppLog.T
 32вЖТimport org.wordpress.android.util.NetworkUtilsWrapper
 33вЖТimport org.wordpress.android.util.analytics.AnalyticsTrackerWrapper
 34вЖТimport org.wordpress.android.util.skip
 35вЖТimport org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.helpers.ConnectionStatus
 36вЖТimport javax.inject.Inject
 37вЖТimport javax.inject.Named
 38вЖТimport javax.inject.Singleton
 39вЖТimport kotlin.coroutines.CoroutineContext
 40вЖТ
 41вЖТ/**
 42вЖТ * Automatically remote-auto-save or upload all local modifications to posts.
 43вЖТ *
 44вЖТ * Auto-uploads happen when the app is placed in the foreground or when the internet connection is restored. In
 45вЖТ * addition to this, call sites can also request an immediate execution by calling [checkConnectionAndUpload].
 46вЖТ *
 47вЖТ * The method [activateAutoUploading] must be called once, preferably during app creation, for the auto-uploads to work.
 48вЖТ */
 49вЖТ@Singleton
 50вЖТ@OpenForTesting
 51вЖТclass UploadStarter @Inject constructor(
 52вЖТ    /**
 53вЖТ     * The Application context
 54вЖТ     */
 55вЖТ    private val context: Context,
 56вЖТ    private val dispatcher: Dispatcher,
 57вЖТ    private val postStore: PostStore,
 58вЖТ    private val pageStore: PageStore,
 59вЖТ    private val siteStore: SiteStore,
 60вЖТ    private val uploadActionUseCase: UploadActionUseCase,
 61вЖТ    private val tracker: AnalyticsTrackerWrapper,
 62вЖТ    @Named(BG_THREAD) private val bgDispatcher: CoroutineDispatcher,
 63вЖТ    @Named(IO_THREAD) private val ioDispatcher: CoroutineDispatcher,
 64вЖТ    private val uploadServiceFacade: UploadServiceFacade,
 65вЖТ    private val networkUtilsWrapper: NetworkUtilsWrapper,
 66вЖТ    private val connectionStatus: LiveData<ConnectionStatus>
 67вЖТ) : CoroutineScope {
 68вЖТ    private val job = Job()
 69вЖТ
 70вЖТ    /**
 71вЖТ     * When the app comes to foreground both `queueUploadFromAllSites` and `queueUploadFromSite` are invoked.
 72вЖТ     * The problem is that they can run in parallel and `uploadServiceFacade.isPostUploadingOrQueued(it)` might return
 73вЖТ     * out-of-date result and a same post is added twice.
 74вЖТ     */
 75вЖТ    private val mutex = Mutex()
 76вЖТ
 77вЖТ    override val coroutineContext: CoroutineContext get() = job + bgDispatcher
 78вЖТ
 79вЖТ    /**
 80вЖТ     * The hook for making this class automatically launch uploads whenever the app is placed in the foreground.
 81вЖТ     */
 82вЖТ    private val processLifecycleObserver = object : DefaultLifecycleObserver {
 83вЖТ        override fun onStart(owner: LifecycleOwner) {
 84вЖТ            queueUploadFromAllSites()
 85вЖТ        }
 86вЖТ    }
 87вЖТ
 88вЖТ    /**
 89вЖТ     * Activates the necessary observers for this class to start auto-uploading.
 90вЖТ     *
 91вЖТ     * This must be called during [org.wordpress.android.WordPress]' creation like so:
 92вЖТ     *
 93вЖТ     * ```
 94вЖТ     * mUploadStarter.activateAutoUploading(ProcessLifecycleOwner.get())
 95вЖТ     * ```
 96вЖТ     */
 97вЖТ    fun activateAutoUploading(processLifecycleOwner: ProcessLifecycleOwner) {
 98вЖТ        // We're skipping the first emitted value because the processLifecycleObserver below will also trigger an
 99вЖТ        // immediate upload.
100вЖТ        connectionStatus.skip(1).observe(processLifecycleOwner, Observer {
101вЖТ            queueUploadFromAllSites()
102вЖТ        })
103вЖТ
104вЖТ        processLifecycleOwner.lifecycle.addObserver(processLifecycleObserver)
105вЖТ    }
106вЖТ
107вЖТ    fun queueUploadFromAllSites() = launch {
108вЖТ        val sites = siteStore.sites
109вЖТ        try {
110вЖТ            checkConnectionAndUpload(sites = sites)
111вЖТ        } catch (e: Exception) {
112вЖТ            AppLog.e(T.MEDIA, e)
113вЖТ        }
114вЖТ    }
115вЖТ
116вЖТ    /**
117вЖТ     * Upload all local drafts from the given [site].
118вЖТ     */
119вЖТ    fun queueUploadFromSite(site: SiteModel) = launch {
120вЖТ        try {
121вЖТ            checkConnectionAndUpload(sites = listOf(site))
122вЖТ        } catch (e: Exception) {
123вЖТ            AppLog.e(T.MEDIA, e)
124вЖТ        }
125вЖТ    }
126вЖТ
127вЖТ    /**
128вЖТ     * If there is an internet connection, uploads all posts with local changes belonging to [sites].
129вЖТ     *
130вЖТ     * This coroutine will suspend until all the [upload] operations have completed. If one of them fails, all query
131вЖТ     * and queuing attempts ([upload]) will be canceled. The exception will be thrown by this method.
132вЖТ     */
133вЖТ    private suspend fun checkConnectionAndUpload(sites: List<SiteModel>) = coroutineScope {
134вЖТ        if (!networkUtilsWrapper.isNetworkAvailable()) {
135вЖТ            return@coroutineScope
136вЖТ        }
137вЖТ
138вЖТ        sites.forEach {
139вЖТ            launch(ioDispatcher) {
140вЖТ                upload(site = it)
141вЖТ            }
142вЖТ        }
143вЖТ    }
144вЖТ
145вЖТ    /**
146вЖТ     * This is meant to be used by [checkConnectionAndUpload] only.
147вЖТ     */
148вЖТ    private suspend fun upload(site: SiteModel) = coroutineScope {
149вЖТ        try {
150вЖТ            mutex.withLock {
151вЖТ                val posts = async { postStore.getPostsWithLocalChanges(site) }
152вЖТ                val pages = async { pageStore.getPagesWithLocalChanges(site) }
153вЖТ                val list = posts.await() + pages.await()
154вЖТ
155вЖТ                list.asSequence()
156вЖТ                    .map { post ->
157вЖТ                        val action = uploadActionUseCase.getAutoUploadAction(post, site)
158вЖТ                        Pair(post, action)
159вЖТ                    }
160вЖТ                    .filter { (_, action) ->
161вЖТ                        action != DO_NOTHING
162вЖТ                    }
163вЖТ                    .toList()
164вЖТ                    .forEach { (post, action) ->
165вЖТ                        trackAutoUploadAction(action, post.status, post.isPage)
166вЖТ                        AppLog.d(
167вЖТ                            AppLog.T.POSTS,
168вЖТ                            "UploadStarter for post (isPage: ${post.isPage}) title: ${post.title}, action: $action"
169вЖТ                        )
170вЖТ                        dispatcher.dispatch(
171вЖТ                            UploadActionBuilder.newIncrementNumberOfAutoUploadAttemptsAction(
172вЖТ                                post
173вЖТ                            )
174вЖТ                        )
175вЖТ                        uploadServiceFacade.uploadPost(
176вЖТ                            context = context,
177вЖТ                            post = post,
178вЖТ                            trackAnalytics = false
179вЖТ                        )
180вЖТ                    }
181вЖТ            }
182вЖТ        } catch (e: CancellationException) {
183вЖТ            AppLog.e(T.MEDIA, e)
184вЖТ            // Do any needed actions while we are still holding the mutex lock, then release it and rethrow the
185вЖТ            // exception so it can be handled upstream
186вЖТ            mutex.unlock()
187вЖТ            throw e
188вЖТ        }
189вЖТ    }
190вЖТ
191вЖТ    private fun trackAutoUploadAction(
192вЖТ        action: UploadAction,
193вЖТ        status: String,
194вЖТ        isPage: Boolean
195вЖТ    ) {
196вЖТ        tracker.track(
197вЖТ            if (isPage) Stat.AUTO_UPLOAD_PAGE_INVOKED else Stat.AUTO_UPLOAD_POST_INVOKED,
198вЖТ            mapOf(
199вЖТ                "upload_action" to action.toString(),
200вЖТ                "post_status" to status
201вЖТ            )
202вЖТ        )
203вЖТ    }
204вЖТ}
205вЖТ

content.text:
Now I understand the issue clearly. Looking at the code:

1. Line 150 uses `mutex.withLock { ... }` which internally handles locking and unlocking with a try/finally block
2. Lines 182-188 catch `CancellationException` and explicitly call `mutex.unlock()` on line 186

The problem is that `withLock` already handles unlocking in its internal finally block. When a `CancellationException` occurs:
1. The `withLock` internal finally block unlocks the mutex
2. Then the catch block catches the exception and calls `mutex.unlock()` again
3. This double-unlock causes the "This mutex is not locked" `IllegalStateException`

The fix is to remove the explicit `mutex.unlock()` call on line 186 since `withLock` already handles unlocking properly. The `withLock` function:
1. Only unlocks if the lock was successfully acquired
2. Uses try/finally to ensure unlocking happens even on exceptions/cancellation

Let me implement the fix:

content.text:
The fix has been successfully implemented. 

## Summary of the Fix

**Root Cause:** The crash `IllegalStateException: This mutex is not locked` was caused by calling `mutex.unlock()` explicitly in the `catch (e: CancellationException)` block while also using `mutex.withLock { ... }` which already handles unlocking internally via a try/finally block.

When a `CancellationException` occurred:
1. The `withLock` function's internal finally block would unlock the mutex
2. Then the catch block would catch the exception and call `mutex.unlock()` again
3. This double-unlock caused the crash

**Solution:** Removed the explicit `mutex.unlock()` call on line 186. The `withLock` function already properly handles unlocking:
- It only unlocks if the lock was successfully acquired
- It uses try/finally to ensure unlocking happens even on exceptions/cancellation
- It handles the cancellation case properly without requiring manual intervention

The fix is minimal and targeted, removing only the problematic line while preserving the logging and re-throwing behavior for cancellation exceptions. No new lint errors were introduced.
